violations.text: Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview of PIC, steak and chicken was placed hot and wrapped in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep. Chicken and steak were unwrapped and placed on sheet pans to finish cooling at the time of inspection. Proper cooling methods reviewed with PIC. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/16/2026

The door to the outside, located in the kitchen and main entrance, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 02/23/2026
The walk-in cooler handle was observed in poor repair. Equipment must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/16/2026

The floor/stairs in the basement area have an accumulation of soil residue, droppings, and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 02/16/2026
No person certified in food safety was present as needed at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 01/29/2024

Pasta salad and cannolis were observed at 48, 46, and 45 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per interview of employee, food was prepared today. Foods were placed in walk-in cooler to blast chill. Deli display case was observed with no doors. TCS foods cannot be placed in this unit until internal temperatures of food can be maintained 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/29/2024

Working containers of sauces and dry ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 02/02/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris: 1. speedracks in walk-in cooler 2. shelves in speedboxes in walk-in cooler 3. fanguards in walk-in cooler New Violation. Correct By: 02/02/2024

Employees' clothing or other personal belongings were observed stored in the kitchen area. Lockers or other suitable facilities shall be used for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and other possessions. New Violation. Correct By: 01/29/2024
